message:
refactor(tests): hoist per-command TEST_DIR_LOCK into shared util::test_helpers module

Six cli/commands/*.rs test modules each declared their own
identical
    static TEST_DIR_LOCK: Mutex<()> = Mutex::new(());
to serialize tests that mutated process-wide state (`set_current_dir`
via DirGuard). Each module's lock was independent of the others, so
running tests across modules in parallel could still race on the
shared `cwd`.

Hoist a single shared lock into `src/util/mod.rs`:
    #[cfg(test)]
    pub mod test_helpers {
        use std::sync::{LazyLock, Mutex};
        pub static TEST_DIR_LOCK: LazyLock<Mutex<()>> = LazyLock::new(|| Mutex::new(()));
    }
and rewrite every callsite from
    let _lock = TEST_DIR_LOCK.lock().unwrap();
to
    let _lock = crate::util::test_helpers::TEST_DIR_LOCK.lock().unwrap();
in:
  - src/cli/commands/agents.rs
  - src/cli/commands/close.rs
  - src/cli/commands/defer.rs
  - src/cli/commands/info.rs
  - src/cli/commands/reopen.rs
  - src/cli/commands/where.rs
The per-file `static TEST_DIR_LOCK: Mutex<()>` declarations and
the matching `use std::sync::Mutex;` imports are removed. Each
file now serializes against the *same* lock, so cross-module
parallel runs no longer race.

Cargo.lock left modified intentionally — same local-sibling-path
patch override situation flagged in the previous beads_rust
passes. Untracked helper script `fix_test_locks.sh` (a one-off
sed-driven mass-rename) is intentionally not staged.
